content:
How can I get this fixed ? Haven't received much help on mailing list yet for this :(

https://lore.kernel.org/all/5c93f6aaff0bcbc907d14d3a74ee5073fa135a07.1736766672.git.viresh.kumar@linaro.org/
The macro probably needs to do a search-n-replace from - to _ for the variables using the module name.
Is the hyphen required? I can help or do it (the file name with hyphens also came up recently again), but I may take a bit to get to it.
In my usecase, there is at least one existing platform device which is named "cpufreq-dt".

Did a quick search:
$ git grep platform_device_register.*-

and there are a lot of such devices.
Yeah, there are many uses of hyphens, but do you need the hyphen for your particular case?
I assume you want to have the same one for equivalence, or is there another reason?

I have written cpufreq-dt driver in Rust, whose platform device is created from code (at various places in kernel). And I absolutely need it, unless I want to change all occurances of cpufreq-dt in existing kernel.

One of the examples of device creation:

https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git/tree/drivers/cpufreq/cpufreq-dt-platdev.c#n233
My Rust driver won't get probed without this :(
Ah, sorry, yeah -- we don't want to change existing occurrences at all.
Are you planning to get it into the next merge window? If so, I can look at it to give you something for that.
I was waiting for Acks (received none yet).. If someone can provide them (which can be done as a lot of review is already done), I can get it merged in this merge window only.
Do you mean the one currently ongoing? That is way too late, even if a patch to solve that was already in the list for a while. Or you mean a subset of those patches?

Yeah, it is late now. Just because it was all new stuff (and likely won't break any existing usercases), I was overly optimistic of still trying to get it merged. But more realisticly, I can push it for linux-next once rc1 is out and get it merged in the next cycle.

So fixing the hyphen issue, along with Acks for rest of the series will be really helpful..
I may be confused, but there is no patch out for that yet, so that seems optimistic. Plus, doesn't Greg want to pick part of your patches for 6.14 anyway?

Alice Ryhl said:

The macro probably needs to do a search-n-replace from - to _ for the variables using the module name.

I just tested the replace on the name locally, it's trivial and makes a simple example work.

But your driver @Viresh Kumar also depends on unmerged patches right? AFAIU the platform device abstraction isn't upstream yet.
I think they will be in -rc1, and I see Greg picked that for -rc1, so Viresh could send v8 on top of -rc1 (but I think he meant sending it soon based on -next instead so that it is ready by -rc1).
Then v8 could be reviewed and applied, e.g. around -rc3.
I tried to send a patch to address this, but based it on the wrong branch, please ignore :-/
Again, sorry for spamming your inboxes :-/ https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250122133952.501055-1-anisse@astier.eu/

Miguel Ojeda said:

I may be confused, but there is no patch out for that yet, so that seems optimistic. Plus, doesn't Greg want to pick part of your patches for 6.14 anyway?

Sorry for causing the confusion. I wasn't talking about the fix for the hyphen issue, but my series in general that adds cpufreq/OPP Rust support.

Greg has picked only one of the patches for now (OF specific) and there are no pending comments on others though. Its not very clear if I can get the series merged in current merge window or not. If this can be part of 6.14, it would be good, one less item on my plate. I will give it a try post rc1.

Yes, all dependencies will be part of rc1.
It's too late for 6.14

Its not very clear if I can get the series merged in current merge window or not.

Every subsystem is different, of course, but even if you ignore review time and linux-next time, you have dependencies, so you would need to justify a cross-merge too.

For the Rust subsystem, it would be more than 2 weeks late for a series like this (https://rust-for-linux.com/contributing#key-cycle-dates).
i.e. it is your series and your subsystem, so it is up to you, of course -- but it is quite surprising to me!
By the way, I just noticed there is no MAINTAINERS entry for the cpumask.rs file -- not sure if that is intended. I will send a quick comment to the list.
I think there will be some objection, so 6.15 works better. I will queue it for linux-next until then to get more testing.. Year, I missed entries for cpumask and clk.rs.. Will add them.

Anisse Astier said:

I tried to send a patch to address this, but based it on the wrong branch, please ignore :-/

Should I include your patch in my series (v8) now? Or will you get it merged separately ?

Viresh Kumar said:

Anisse Astier said:

I tried to send a patch to address this, but based it on the wrong branch, please ignore :-/

Should I include your patch in my series (v8) now? Or will you get it merged separately ?

I don't even know if it's acceptable. Feel free to include the v2 in your next iteration if it hasn't had any comments by then.

v8 wouldn't work without it, no? i.e. it cannot be really be applied to -next without it. So either v8 is based on that patch, or it includes the patch.

Or am I still misunderstanding?
As for merging it, it can be done in two trees sharing a branch, but unless someone else needs this (i.e. hyphens) in that cycle, it can just go through Viresh's.
To clarify: yes, do include the patch. I just saw this message today, and did not realize the series was 9 days old. So I hadn't realized you would send v8 very soon™.

Anisse Astier said:

To clarify: yes, do include the patch. I just saw this message today, and did not realize the series was 9 days old. So I hadn't realized you would send v8 very soon™.

I was thinking about sending the next version after 2 weeks of V7, so that would be next week. This is the first time I raised the issue on zulip, but my series always had the hack and I did ask for help in the cover-letter. Good that there is a fix availalbe now, thanks for that.

I will carry your patch in my series now. Will send the next version after rc1 is out, that would also give others to review your change.
